content.full_text:
1750 Scenic View Court  
San Leandro, California 94577  
351-8338  
June 19, 1976

Mr. Alfredo Flores  
Fair Political Practices Commission  
1100 Kay St.  
Sacramento, CA 95814  

Re: Olive H. Mayer  

Dear Al:

The letter from Mr. Gyemant regarding the above-captioned Councilperson for Woodside raises some interesting problems with which the Commission should wrestle.

Rereading Section 82030, the last sentence of the first paragraph refers to "any business entity or trust." Since Section 82005 deals with the definition of "business entity" and includes business trust, I must question the construction of the sentence in Section 82030. There are other types of trusts besides business trusts.

It is possible that Mrs. Mayer is the beneficiary of a "blind trust" or she may be the beneficiary of a "Clifford Trust" or some other type of reversionary trust. A testamentary trust would raise other questions as would a life estate in a trust.

I am not criticizing your response to Mr. Gyemant. I would merely like to bring to your attention the fact that the subject of trusts cannot be dismissed by relying on Section 81004.

I would like to discuss it with you at further length and I would request that the next time a similar question is presented, it should receive the full attention of the Commission.

Sincerely,

Patricia Lapan
